observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to properly recognize the semi-expendable items issued during the year and in the prior years, to present fairly the balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management:
ensure that budget allocation for specific MOOE account is carefully studied and projected based on historical data and anticipated needs during budget preparation to have a reliable measurement base for fund utilization; and
in cases where the budget allocation for a specific MOOE is found to be insufficient, consider the applicability of fund augmentation, following the provisions of Section 336 of RA 7160 to have a flexible and adaptable budget; and
moving forward, ensure that all MOOE transactions are properly classified in the specific or appropriate MOOE account and use the Other MOE account to record expenses not falling under any of the specific MOOE accounts, for the fair presentation of account balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management cause the preparation and submission of the monthly BRS, in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-011 to ensure timely resolution of discrepancies, improved accuracy of financial records, and adherence to regulatory guidelines.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Accountant to maintain SLs for all receivable accounts, in compliance with the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445, to enhance the collection efficiency of the LGU and to established the accuracy, completeness and fair presentation of the receivable accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended Management to facilitate fair presentation of accounts in the financial statement and:
instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that future advance payments should be recorded under Advances to Contractors account and only transferred to CIP accounts upon recoupment; and
ensure timely recoupment of advance payments in compliance with the provisions of RA No. 9184 by implementing a systematic recoupment schedule of advances to contractors.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal GSO and the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 114 and 121 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs particularly on the recognition of inventory and related inventory expense, to ensure reliable financial reporting, and strengthen internal control over the receipt, issuance, and utilization of inventories.

We recommended the Management to implement the use of the appropriate forms to record and monitor its PPE and Semi-Expendable Property as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2024-006, ensuring compliance with the updated guidelines and improve the accuracy of asset records, as well as enhance the accountability and traceability of all its property.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC and concerned procurement staff to ensure that all CSEs are procured through PS-DBM, as mandated, in faithful observance of the related procurement rules and regulations, otherwise, secure CNAS for the procurement of CSE from alternative sources.

We recommended that the Management maximize the utilization of the 20% DF by accelerating the implementation of pending projects, identifying the root causes of non-implementation, and developing practical, targeted solutions to ensure the effective execution of developmental initiatives. Furthermore, management should be diligent, thorough, and precise in evaluating the timely completion of each project to ensure their successful implementation within the targeted year.

We recommended that Management:
a. 	ensure the timely and proper utilization of the LDRRMF by strengthening the planning, implementation, and monitoring mechanisms for disaster risk reduction and management programs, to enhance the LGU’s preparedness and response capabilities; and
b. 	require the concerned personnel to prepare the LDRRMF Investment Plan annually following the prescribed format, as shown in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, for the effective monitoring and evaluation of the utilization of LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Management direct the MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the monthly submission requirements and ensure that the required reports are submitted on time to the COA Auditor and furnished to the OCD and DILG-LGOO, to facilitate timely oversight and promote transparency, accountability and effective monitoring of the LDRRMF utilization.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to reevaluate the recording of the receipt and utilization of shares from tobacco excise taxes, record the necessary adjusting entries and, moving forward, ensure the proper recording of financial transactions in the books, for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to reevaluate the recording of the utilization of funds received from NGAs and other LGUs to record the necessary adjusting entries and, moving forward, ensure the proper recording of financial transactions in the books, particularly on the receipts, utilizations and liquidations of fund transfers, for the fair presentation of the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the cost of completed projects recorded under the CIP- Infrastructure Assets and CIP-Buildings and Other Structures to Other Infrastructure Assets and Buildings account, respectively, when necessary; and determine the corresponding depreciation expense to be recognized in the books.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
make the necessary adjustments to record the amount of retention monies with the affected PPE accounts and correspondingly, adjust the related accumulated depreciation. Likewise, record the total amount of retention monies under the Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable account; and
henceforth, record the cost of the PPE at its full cost and record the corresponding retention money withheld to the Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable account to ensure fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management require the Accounting Office to exert best effort to locate relevant documents supporting the balances of the aforementioned receivable and payable accounts, that would facilitate the reconstruction of their respective SLs, in order to establish the reliability and fair presentation of these accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and GSO to prepare the PPELC and PC, respectively, in compliance with the aforementioned provisions or, due to the time-intensive nature of this process, designate personnel from respective offices to focus exclusively on the task, to facilitate the reconciliation of both records and ensure fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
through the concerned offices, after the completion of the ledger cards for PPE, immediately conduct the reconciliation of records to identify the cause of the discrepancies between the balances of the records being maintained by the Accounting Office and the GSO or Inventory Committee.
instruct the Inventory Committee to immediately complete the physical count of all PPE of the Municipality and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Audit Team; and
study and consider the applicability of COA Circular No. 2020-006 which provides the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of properties for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, to come-up with a complete RPCPPE that will help both the Accounting Office and the GSO in updating and reconciling their records; thus, establishing the fair presentation of the PPE account in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Management require:
the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amounts due and collectible, based on updated Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s Index Card to ensure an accurate and reliable basis in setting up the Receivables from RPT and SET;
the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books to reconcile the balances of RPT Receivables, Deferred RPT, SET Receivables and Deferred RPT with the list provided by the Municipal Treasurer; and
the Municipal Accountant, moving forward, to recognize the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the submitted duly certified list of taxpayers by the Municipal Treasurer with the amount due and collectible for the year and maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers for proper accounting and monitoring of tax collections and delinquencies.

We recommended that the Management:
Record the on-going projects in the total amount of P2,299,087.64 in the books of accounts to present fairly the Construction in Progress accounts;and
Require the Municipal Accountant and Engineering Office to establish a reporting mechanism on the status of on-going constructions-in-progress and completed projects to prevent delays in recognition of the same to their appropriate accounts.

We recommended that the Management immediately create an Inventory Committee and speed up the conduct of physical inventory of all PPEs of the Municipality in order to finalize the preparation of the RPCPPE pursuant to Section 66 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System and COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare necessary adjusting entries to record the vaccines received and the related distribution to constituents in order to reflect the correct balance of the accounts presented in the financial statements.
Henceforth, the Municipal Mayor require all departments and offices to coordinate with the Municipal Accounting Office for all donations received for proper recording and accounting thereof in compliance with Paragraphs          96 and 97 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 23 and Revised Chart of Accounts.

We recommended that the Management:
Reconcile the discrepancy of P843,524.17 from the reported balance of the account in the financial statements of P553,779.12 against the report maintained by the Team of P1,397,303.29; and
Submit the documents evidencing the full/partial settlement of some disallowances for proper evaluation and preparation or issuance of NSSDC, if warranted; and
Maintain and properly update separate subsidiary ledgers for each disallowance and for each official/employee determined to be liable/responsible for the amount disallowed to properly monitor the outstanding balance of the disallowances in compliance with COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare final liquidation/terminal reports of these eight (8) fund transfers from various NGAs and submit to the concerned source agencies for proper accounting thereof; and
Return the unutilized balance of P500,816.63 to the Source Agency and adjust the same in the books of accounts to fully settle the eight (8) accounts.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Accounting Office to settle the amount of P569,052.04, if these are valid claims, if not revert to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund pursuant to Section 98 of the PD No. 1445 to ensure fair presentation of the Accounts Payable account in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer the unexpended balances of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE amounting to P2,484,308.00 and P2,522,961.00, respectively to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities-DRRMF” in the Trust Fund books pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Section 21 of Republic Act No. 10121, to present fairly the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management direct the LDRRM Office to expedite the implementation of the Programs, Projects, and Activities of the Municipality to optimize the utilization of disaster risk reduction and management fund intended for disaster risk mitigation.

We recommended that the Management speed up the implementation of the six (6) infrastructure projects in the total amount of  P7,023,413.00 funded out of the 20% DF as per Annual Investment Plan and that closer coordination among the officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring be undertaken to achieve the desired level of outcomes.

We recommended that Management intensify tax collections thru the formulation of strategies to improve collection of real property taxes and encourage the involvement of barangay officials in the tax campaign to reach out to a larger area, pursuant to Section 256 of the Local Government Code of the Philippines.

We recommended that the Management comply strictly with the prescribed controls over PPE as provided for in pertinent laws, rules and regulations. We also recommended to Management the following: Create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all PPEs of the LGU, whether acquired through purchase or donation, including those constructed by administration and found at station pursuant to COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020. Update the property records based on the results of physical inventory, and reconcile with accounting records thru the Inventory Committee created. Undertake all other procedures prescribed under COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to come up with the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the updated ledger cards for each item of the PPE after the inventory taking pursuant to the Section 269 of the GAM for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The GSO, in coordination with the Property Division, is currently planning on how to conduct and complete the physical inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile with the Accountants of the different Source Agencies for all funds received to determine the correct balances still due for liquidation. Require the Municipal Accountant to review all existing fund transfers and return all unused or unexpended balance upon completion of the project, if any.
observations.managementAction: Still on the process of reviewing all existing fund transfers to return all unused balance upon reconciliation with the Source Agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal GSO or the Property/Supply Officer: Submit the required report and its supporting documents as required in COA Circular No. 2020-009 to ascertain whether the donations in-kind received by the LGU are properly accounted for. Comply with the reporting guidelines stated in Item 1.b.1 of the same Circular for submission to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, through the Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnished the COA Auditors.
observations.managementAction: The Management is currently preparing the necessary reports required.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant:
Make a journal entry to adjust the outstanding checks which are not yet stale that were recorded as accounts payable in the amount of P8,820,695.30; and
Revert to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund the accounts payable amounting to P1,347,524.43 which are unidentified and no actual claim has been filed as prescribed by Section 98 of PD 1445.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant analyze the Receivables accounts, Accounts Receivable, Due from NGAs and Due from LGUs amounting to P8,147.39, P47,259.63 and P118,080.06, respectively, which has been dormant for 13 years and make the necessary verification against previous financial reports and schedules to determine the details of these accounts and confirm their balances, if warranted.  Otherwise, submit request for authority to write off the accounts to COA following the procedures set under COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor the implementation of the projects and utilization of the funds and submit liquidation thereof and/ return/remit to the Source Agency any unused/unutilized balances pursuant to to COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 19, 1994; and
Submit to COA the request for write-off of dormant accounts in the amount of P160,457.81 following the procedures set under COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the payments on the progress billing of on-going infrastructure projects erroneously recorded as PPE instead of Construction in Progress in the amount of P3,998,620.22 pursuant to Sec. 50(4) of the New Government Accounting System, Volume I.

We recommended that Management:
Create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all PPEs of the Municipality in order to finalize the preparation of the RPCPPE pursuant to Section 66 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System and COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 ; and
Hire job order personnel, if necessary, to assist in the conduct of physical count of PPE for a speedy accomplishment and submission of the RPCPPE.

We recommended that Management:
Reclassify those cash advances granted to those officers and employees who are already dead, no longer connected with the LGU and those whereabouts are not known to “Other Receivables” account in the total amount of P330,683.93;
Require the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to the officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances in the amount of P978,600.00 and demand the immediate settlement thereof; and
Require for write-off of dormant accounts, which has been unliquidated for 11 to 20 years, where liquidation is nil following the procedures set under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure submission of the complete set of financial statements to facilitate review of the transactions of the LGU’s economic enterprise in compliance with Section 313, Article II of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 159 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, to disclose the reconciliation of actual amounts and budgeted amounts in the Notes to Financial Statements and submit the SCBAA on time, and automatically include in the submission of the financial statements in compliance with Paragraph 21 of IPSAS 1 and Paragraph 24 of IPSAS 24, to achieve transparency in financial reporting timely review of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management require:
The Municipal Accountant record all inventories as asset upon delivery and acceptance; and
The Supply Officer to conduct the periodic physical count of supplies and materials every semester and prepare and submit the corresponding Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) pursuant to Section 124, Chapter 7 of the New Government Accounting System for LGUs.

We recommended that Management:
Maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund by speeding up the implementation of the above projects amounting to P9,400,185.97 and identify causes of non-implementation and to develop feasible and specific solutions to effectively implement developmental projects; and
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to be very prudent, meticulous, conscientious and precise in evaluating the timely implementation of each and every project.  Likewise, he should also conduct regular inspection of the projects so that the accomplishment is monitored and evaluated based on the project implementation schedule as stated in the Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that Management:
Optimally use the allocation of the DRRMF, through the full implementation of the projects and activities allowed and mentioned in Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1, to build a disaster resilient community and to reduce disaster risks; and
Direct the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to monitor the extent of the implementation of the LDRRMFIP to ensure that the public receive the benefits that may accrue from the utilization of the fund.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management: Require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate preparation of adjusting journal entries to identify and record all reconciling items identified in the Bank Reconciliation Statements.  Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the stale checks after exhaustion of all remedies such as written notices to the payees and the corresponding DVs and supporting documents.  If cancellation is not possible despite written notices, the corresponding amounts of the stale checks may be reverted back to the book balances with the recording of accounts payable.
observations.managementAction: Identified the nature of the reconciling items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements and adjusted the stale checks.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Collecting Officers turn over their collections intact daily or not later than the next working day to the Municipal Treasurer pursuant to Section 63, Volume 1 of the Local Government Accounting Manual.
observations.managementAction: Remitted collections daily to the Liquidating Officer for immediate deposit to LGU’s depository bank.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer include checks in the preparation of the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms pursuant to Sections 51, Chapter 2 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: Included checks in the preparation of the monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC require the winning bidders to submit the appropriate form of certification by the Insurance Commission authorizing the surety or insurance company to issue such performance security, which is specific to every awarded project as required by Section 39.2 of the Revised RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the appropriate form of certification by the Insurance Commission authorizing the surety or insurance company to issue such performance security.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following to the Management: Direct the Municipal Accountant and officials concerned to submit the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit. Ensure that fund transfers with specific purposes are recorded in the Due from LGUs account instead of Subsidy to Other Local Government Units account so that the LGU can monitor the implementation of the programs.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the required documents and justification to the nature of the funds given to the Barangay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant refrain from granting cash advances to an elective official other than for travelling expenses and cash advances should only be granted to permanently appointed Disbursing Officer in consonance to Section 339 of RA 7160 and Item 4.1.4 of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

observations.recommendation: We recommended adherence to the provisions of RA 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations, specifically the observance of the recommended timetable relative to the prescribed period of action on procurement activities to enable timely delivery of goods and services, as well as the benefits by which the intended beneficiaries can derive therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The BAC adhered to the provisions of RA 9184 specifically the observance of the recommended timetable relative to the prescribed period of action on procurement activities.

observations.managementAction: All concerned officials are required to accomplished all the necessary and relevant information required in the Purchase Order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: Require the Accounting Unit to prepare a billing statement to the Officials and Employees concerned totaling P93,102.83. Instruct the Treasury and Accounting Units, upon payment by Officials and Employees of the non-withheld taxes, to remit the same amount to BIR representing the underpayment of taxes. Require the Accounting Unit to ensure proper computation of the amount of taxes to be withheld in accordance with pertinent Revenue Regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to make a detailed analysis of the RPT/SET Receivables and its corresponding reciprocal accounts and to record the adjusting entries pertaining to the unreconciled RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET accounts;    
                                                                                                    
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a complete and accurate list of taxpayers indicating the amount due and collectible for the year, including delinquencies and penalties and to furnish the report to the Municipal Accountant, which shall serve as the basis in setting up the RPT/SET Receivables for the year; and


Intensify tax collections thru the formulation of strategies to improve collection of real property taxes and encourage the involvement of barangay officials in the tax campaign to reach out to a larger area.
observations.managementAction: The reciprocal accounts were already reconciled and the amount of collectibles for the year were set up.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management comply strictly with the prescribed controls over PPE as provided in pertinent laws, rules and regulations and require the following:

The Inventory Team/Committee to conduct an inventory of all the properties of the Municipality and reconcile the related accounting and property records. Discrepancies and/or deficiencies, if any, should be analyzed/investigated. Submit a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the Audit Team concerned as required under Section 66 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System.

The General Services Officer to renew the Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment every three years or every time there is a change in accountability to establish who is in actual possession or custody of all government properties in accordance with Section 56, Vol. II of the New Government Accounting System Manual.

The Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) and Property Cards, respectively, for each class of property, plant and equipment and post all transactions promptly from source documents and reconcile their records regularly in order to establish the accuracy of balance of each PPE account presented in the financial statements as of a certain date.
observations.managementAction: Management is still conducting inventory count of all properties of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Enforce collection and settlement of the long outstanding receivables and cash advances through issuance of demand letters;

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit a sworn Certification for unliquidated cash advances, and other receivables with no available documents but are not yet considered dormant as defined under COA Circular 2016-005; and

Initiate the preparation and filing of the request for authority to write-off accounts with the COA in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to the accountable officers to enforce collection.  The  request for write-off of those receivables that are long outstanding is also being prepared by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort in identifying the discrepancies in the balances of the reciprocal accounts, “Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds”, and to effect the necessary adjustments to bring the affected accounts into agreement.
observations.managementAction: The reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, were already reconciled.

observations.recommendation:

Require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to reconcile their records to correct the discrepancy of the Cash-Local Treasury account.  Henceforth, regular quarterly reconciliation required under Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 should be religiously followed to avoid existence or accumulation of discrepancies and ensure timely correction of errors if needed; and 

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the collections intact and regularly and immediately liquidate all outstanding Petty Cash at the end of the year to avoid misuse, theft or loss of funds pursuant to Section 69(1) of PD No. 1445 and Sections 32 and 48 of the MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Cash - Local Treasury and Petty Cash Fund accounts have no balances at year- end.

Transfer the disbursing function of the Liquidating Officer to another qualified Accountable Officer to avoid conflict of duty;


Instruct the Liquidating Officer to remit intact all the collections to the Municipal Treasurer; and 

Require the Municipal Treasurer to take custody and exercise proper management of the funds of the agency pursuant to the above-cited rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Management designated a new Liquidating Officer.

observations.recommendation:

Require the Municipal Accountant to compute extensively total proceeds that should accrue to SEF against GF since the date it was merged with the latter fund and reconcile the pertinent reciprocal accounts; and

observations.managementAction: Management did not conduct physical count of all properties and submit the said inventory report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management secure copies of the MOA/TA from the source agencies and furnish the COA Audit Team copies of the same to determine whether fund transfers are properly utilized and the requirements for projects implementation and reporting are strictly complied with.

We recommended that the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer facilitate the approval of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan by the National Solid Waste Management Commission and ensure that the programs and activities included in such plan are consistent with the National Solid Waste Management Framework.

We recommended that the Management organize and immediately fill up the required LDRRM Officer and Staff position/s, through the consent of the Legislative Body, and to provide the corresponding amount for salaries in order for the constituents to avail the services of responsible and competent persons in strengthening the LGU's capacity for disaster risk reduction and management.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan Members revisit the explicit provisions of Section 191 of RA 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991, for them to make adjustments/amendments to the Revenue Code of the LGU, which was never been renewed or improved since CY 2002.

We recommended that the Management:
Submit an explanation on the occurrence of the noted negative balances in the Due to NGAs account;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile with the Accountants of the different Source Agencies for all funds received to determine the correct balances still due for liquidation; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to review all existing fund transfers and return all unused or unexpended balance upon completion of the project, if any.

We recommended that the Management require the officers concerned to analyze and evaluate the status of work of each project in the Construction in Progress account and close the same to its appropriate asset account, if warranted.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to make a detailed analysis of the RPT/SET Receivables and its corresponding reciprocal accounts and to record the adjusting entries pertaining to the unreconciled RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET accounts;
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a complete and accurate list of taxpayers indicating the amount due and collectible for the year, including delinquencies and penalties and to furnish the report to the Municipal Accountant, which shall serve as the basis in setting up the RPT/SET Receivables for the year; and
Intensify tax collections thru the formulation of strategies to improve collection of real property taxes and encourage the involvement of barangay officials in the tax campaign to reach out to a larger area.

We recommended that the Management comply strictly with the prescribed controls over PPE as provided in pertinent laws, rules and regulations and require the following:
The Inventory Team/Committee to conduct an inventory of all the properties of the Municipality and reconcile the related accounting and property records. Discrepancies and/or deficiencies, if any, should be analyzed/investigated. Submit a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the Audit Team concerned as required under Section 66 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System.
The General Services Officer to renew the Acknowledgement Receipts for Equipment every three years or every time there is a change in accountability to establish who is in actual possession or custody of all government properties in accordance with Section 56, Vol. II of the New Government Accounting System Manual.
The Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) and Property Cards, respectively, for each class of property, plant and equipment and post all transactions promptly from source documents and reconcile their records regularly in order to establish the accuracy of balance of each PPE account presented in the financial statements as of a certain date.

We recommended that the Management:
Enforce collection and settlement of the long outstanding receivables and cash advances through issuance of demand letters;
Require the Municipal Accountant to submit a sworn Certification for unliquidated cash advances, and other receivables with no available documents but are not yet considered dormant as defined under COA Circular 2016-005; and
Initiate the preparation and filing of the request for authority to write-off accounts with the COA in accordance with COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort in identifying the discrepancies in the balances of the reciprocal accounts, “Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds”, and to effect the necessary adjustments to bring the affected accounts into an agreement.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to reconcile their records to correct the discrepancy of the Cash-Local Treasury account.  Henceforth, regular quarterly reconciliation required under Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 should be religiously followed to avoid existence or accumulation of discrepancies and ensure timely correction of errors if needed; and
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit the collections intact and regularly and immediately liquidate all outstanding Petty Cash at the end of the year to avoid misuse, theft or loss of funds pursuant to Section 69(1) of PD No. 1445 and Sections 32 and 48 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs.

We recommended that the Management conduct physical inventory count of their work/zoo animals, and any increase/decrease in the balances of the account should be accurately reported in the books.  Henceforth, should any loss occur, the proper accountable officer shall file a notice of loss and request for relief from accountability with the Auditor or the Commission, as the case may be.

We recommended that the Management:
Transfer the disbursing function of the Liquidating Officer to another qualified Accountable Officer to avoid conflict of duty;
Instruct the Liquidating Officer to remit intact all the collections to the Municipal Treasurer; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to take custody and exercise proper management of the funds of the agency pursuant to the above-cited rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to compute extensively total proceeds that should accrue to SEF against GF since the date it was merged with the latter fund and reconcile the pertinent reciprocal accounts; and
Require also the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a separate cashbook and depository account for the SEF and deposit the proceeds accruing to SEF its respective depository bank account maintained.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant correct their records for advances for payroll account and religiously reconcile their books of accounts in compliance with Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be more prudent in the preparation of checks, otherwise, habitual errors will be charged against the person responsible.  Moreover, all cancelled checks should be submitted to the Office of the Commission on Audit for review, verification, and custodianship.

We recommended that the Management:
Maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund by speeding up the implementation of the prioritized programs, projects and activities funded out of the 20% DF as per Annual Investment Plan and that closer coordination among the officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring be undertaken to achieve the desired level of outcomes.
Instruct the Municipal Planning Development Officer to immediately look into the unimplemented developmental projects as embodied in the Annual Investment Plan to identify causes of non-implementation and to develop feasible and specific solutions to effectively implement developmental projects.
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to be very prudent, meticulous, conscientious and precise in evaluating the timely implementation of each and every project.  Likewise, he should also conduct regular inspection of the projects so that the accomplishment is monitored and evaluated based on the project implementation schedule as stated in the Annual Investment Plan.
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to submit monthly to the Audit Team a detailed Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations and Balances, showing, among others, the individual programs, projects and activities funded with current and continuing appropriations under the 20% Development Fund and to review the balances of current and continuing appropriations at the end of the year for possible inclusion in the annual budget preparation. Unexpended balances of appropriations of projects, programs and activities, which were already completed/accomplished should be re-appropriated for other PPAs of the ensuing year upon recommendation of the Local Chief Executive and approval of the Sanggunian Bayan.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Consider hiring additional personnel to fully monitor the implementation of the PPAs related to LDRRM;
Optimally use the 70% allocation of the DRRMF, through the full implementation of the projects and activities allowed and mentioned in Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1, to build a disaster resilient community and to reduce disaster risks; and
Direct the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to monitor the extent of the implementation of the LDRRMFIP to ensure that the public receive the benefits that may accrue from the utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the Management advise the Municipal Accountant to remit the taxes withheld on or before the 10th day of the month following the month of withholding, except for taxes withheld for the month of December, which shall be filed/remitted on or before the 15th day of the month of the succeeding year, pursuant to Section 5.116 of Revenue Regulations. Furthermore, ensure that penalties due to late remittance shall be borne by responsible officials and not by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management require the Accounting Section to analyze the above accounts to determine the cause of the outstanding balances and remit immediately the contributions found to be unremitted and make necessary adjustments, if warranted.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following to the Management:

Re-study the procedure in the recording of collections to ensure that all deposits in the LGU's accounts are properly recorded on time and reconciled with bank records;

Exert extra effort in tracing and analyzing the unrecorded deposits and debits and come up with appropriate documents; and

Provide the necessary adjusting entries in the accounting records to update the accounts affected and come up with updated balances.

The Municipal Treasurer should immediately identify the drawers/payors whose checks have been dishonored. observations.recommendation:
8. We recommended that an Inventory Team be created to conduct physical
   count of all properties and prepare  the  necessary  reports.  Also,
   require the Municipal Accountant to prepare  Subsidiary  Ledger  and
   the Supply Officer to reconcile their balances of every PPE  account
   to have a reliable and accurate balances in the financial reports.

7. We reiterated the recommendation that the Municipal Accountant  give
       priority to the preparation and  submission  of  the  required  Bank
       Reconciliation  Statements  for  all  bank  accounts  and  make  the
       necessary adjustments to present the correct balance of Cash in Bank
       account as at  year-end  given  that  the  “Cash  in  Bank”  account
       represents a significant portion of the total assets of the LGU.

4. We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  provide  depreciation
       for the PPE accounts and record in the books to reflect the  correct
       balance thereof in the financial statements.

6. We recommended management to:

      a. direct the Municipal Accountant to adopt the perpetual  inventory
         method for supplies and materials;


      b.  provide  budget  for  storage  for  the   supply   requirements,
         otherwise,  if  no  available  storage  area  is  available,   we
         recommended that control procedures be  established  to  regulate
         the issuance to the end-users within a month; and


      c. a central procurement system maybe introduced by establishing the
         actual needs of each section offices as documented in the  Annual
         Procurement Plan.

7. We recommended that the Local Development Council and the  Municipal
       Planning and Development Office (MPDO) determine causes of the  non-
       implementation  of  projects.   Formulate   and   develop   specific
       strategies  and  solutions  to  effectively  implement   development
       projects. Lastly, consider formulating a timetable within  which  to
       complete the  unimplemented  projects  and  review  the  costing  of
       projects programmed two to seven years ago to conform to the present
       conditions.

8. We recommended that Management require the  former   Municipal
       Treasurer to explain the irregularities on the disbursement of funds
       of the Municipality and to immediately restitute the total amount of
       P500,000.00 without prejudice to the institution  of  administrative
       and/or criminal action.

6. The Treasurer adhered that starting  CY  2018,  the  collectors  and
       tellers shall maintain their respective cashbooks.

5. We recommended that Management comply with the provisions of IRR  of
       RA 10121 and COA  Circular  No.  2012-002  and  instruct  the  LDRRM
       Officer  to  submit  the  report  within  the  required  period   as
       prescribed in Sec. 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

5. We recommended that Management require the Municipal  Accountant  to
       prepare and maintain  subsidiary  ledgers  to  support  the  general
       ledger balances to ensure  fair  presentation  of  accounts  in  the
       financial statements.

6. We recommended Management to maintain a separate subsidiary  ledger
        and financial statements for the special accounts  in  the  General
        Fund Proper to ensure adequate information of  the  individual  sub
        code accounts maintained under the General Fund Proper.

6. We recommended that the Accountable Officers submit  regularly  the
        required Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and
        the Report of Collections and Deposits pursuant to Section  50  and
        51 of Volume II of NGAS Manual and Section 64 of P.D 1445 to ensure
        proper accounting of thereof. Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer was
        required to submit the CRAAF duly  supported  with  the  individual
        RAAF of all collecting officer not later than the 5th  day  of  the
        ensuing month.

6. We recommended the General Service Officer to regularly submit  the
        Monthly Report of Official Travels with  the  accomplished  Drivers
        Trip Ticked for each vehicle and equipment to support  the  Monthly
        Report of Fuel Consumption to the Auditor for the  verification  in
        the audit of fuel, oil and lubricants.

3. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  immediately  transfer
        the unexpended balance of the DRRMF to a special trust  fund  under
        the account “Trust Liability – DRRM” as prescribed by COA  Circular
        No. 2012-002. Consequently, the account shall  be  used  to  record
        transfers of the agency’s unutilized QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of  the
        previous years, receipts of NDRRMF, DRRMF from other LGUs and other
        sources.

        We further recommended that  subsidiary  ledgers  for  transfer  of
        agency’s unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund be  maintained.

9. We recommended that the LGU GFPS set-up and maintain GAD  database
        and  develop GAD Codes  in order to  adhere  to  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
        Joint  Memorandum  Circular  Nos.  2013-01  and  2016-01  in   the
        implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities.

3. We reiterate therefore,  the  submission  of  copies  of  Purchase
         Orders and contracts together with the other  documents  mentioned
         in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.


      4. We recommended management to comply strictly with the provision of
         in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.   Otherwise,
         non-submission of the required documents will cause the  immediate
         suspension of salaries of person responsible until  complied  with
         the requirement pursuant to Section  122  of  Presidential  Decree
         1445.
